title_en: How realistic is Andy Burnham’s council-house building pledge?
title_zh: 安迪·伯纳姆提出的市政住房建设承诺有多现实？
rubric_en: It would be far better if Britain’s prime-minister-in-waiting followed “Manchesterism”
rubric_zh: 如果英国“待选首相”能走向“曼彻斯特主义”，局面将会好得多

paragraphs.en: The “People’s House” in Desford, Leicestershire, was a humble home. With three small bedrooms and hot water heated by a coal fire, in 1952 it was the Conservative government’s model solution to Britain’s post-war housing crisis. Taking just 12 weeks and £952 to build (£24,500, or $33,000, in today’s prices), the house was rented from the council for 16 shillings a week, a 30% market discount and equivalent to about 10% of average manual wages.
paragraphs.zh: 莱斯特郡德斯福德的“人民之家”是一座朴素的小屋。它只有三个小卧室，厨房的热水靠煤火加热；在1952年，这套房屋是保守党政府对英国战后住房危机的示范性解决方案。建造这间房子只用了12周，花费952英镑（按今天的物价约为2.45万英镑，或3.3万美元），由市政部门出租给住户，周租金16先令，相当于市场价的七折，也大约等于普通体力劳动者平均工资的10%。

paragraphs.en: Andy Burnham, the prime-minister-in-waiting, is nostalgic about these post-war council homes, which he thinks used to be “the foundation for everything”. In a speech on June 29th he asked: “If you don’t give people a good home, what chance have they got of having a good life?” One of his few bold policy promises was to “oversee the biggest council-housebuilding programme since the post-war period”.
paragraphs.zh: 即将出任英国首相的安迪·伯纳姆对这些战后市政住房充满怀旧情结，他认为这些房屋曾经是“万事之基”。在6月29日的一次演讲中，他问道：如果不让人们住上体面的房子，他们还有机会过上体面的生活吗？他少有的大胆政策承诺之一是“监管并推动自战后以来最大的市政住宅建设计划”。

paragraphs.en: But council housing is not the only way to give people a good home. Sir Keir Starmer, the outgoing prime minister, focused on enabling private housebuilding, through planning reform, rather than the state picking up the shovel. The most sensible thing for Mr Burnham to do would be to ease planning restrictions further and to relax regulations, such as environmental requirements. Instead, he has said that “undue weight” has been placed on planning reform—he wants to return housing to its post-war heyday of state-led intervention.
paragraphs.zh: 但市政住房并不是让民众获得体面居所的唯一方式。前任首相基尔·斯塔默把重点放在通过规划改革来促进私人住房建设，而不是国家直接拿起铁锹下场盖房。对伯纳姆来说，更明智的做法是进一步放宽规划限制，并放松一些规制，例如环保要求。相反，他说政府在规划改革上“被赋予了过重的分量”，并且他想要把住房建设拉回到战后后期由国家主导干预的繁荣阶段。

paragraphs.en: In 1952, the year the People’s House was built, local authorities funded the construction of four in five of the 200,000 new homes built in England that year. A little under half of housebuilding was public for most of the pre-Thatcher decades. Last year, by contrast, only 12,500 of the 190,000 new homes, or one in 16, were built for what is now called “social rent” (the tenure Mr Burnham seems to have in mind when he talks of council houses), priced at 50% of the market rate on average. Mr Burnham’s announcement implies that he wants to build 75,000 social-rent homes a year, as that was the record level achieved after Margaret Thatcher won power in 1979, often seen as the end of the post-war period. As well as being costly it would take years to reach that level.
paragraphs.zh: 在1952年，也就是“人民之家”建成的那一年，地方当局承担了当年英格兰20万套新建住房中的四分之五。大多数撒切尔时代之前的几十年里，住房建设的近一半仍由公共部门完成。相比之下，去年在190,000套新房中仅有12,500套，也就是每16套中的1套，被归为现在所称的“社会租赁”（即伯纳姆谈论市政房时似乎指的房屋类型），其租金平均只有市场租金的一半。伯纳姆的表态意味着他想每年建造75,000套“社会租赁”住房，因为这正是玛格丽特·撒切尔1979年上台后达到的历史最高水平；这一年份通常被视为战后时期的终点。除此之外，这一目标既昂贵又需要多年才能实现。

paragraphs.en: The promise to build a lot more housing for more people would seem to be a no-brainer. Politicians of all persuasions agree that Britain does not build enough. Between 1955 and 1979 new homes added around 1.9% to the housing stock each year, according to the Centre for Cities, a think-tank. Between 1980 and 2015 that rate fell to 0.8%, the lowest in all but one of 11 other European countries studied.
paragraphs.zh: “建更多房子、供更多人住”这个承诺看起来似乎不言自明。各党派政治家都承认英国建房不足。根据智库“城市之心”（Centre for Cities）的数据，1955年至1979年间，住房存量每年增加约1.9%；而从1980年至2015年下降到0.8%，这在研究的其他11个欧洲国家中几乎是最低水平。

paragraphs.en: Sir Keir’s solution was simple: build more homes. His government set a target that 300,000 homes a year would be built in England—housing policy is devolved to the nations—over its five-year term. He introduced welcome reforms, including a tweak to mandatory housing targets and a change in the rules to give a presumption in favour of development around well-connected railway stations. But some of these reforms won’t be implemented until later this year at the earliest and they are in any case not sufficient. By our calculations, the number of homes added to the stock fell from 222,000 in the year to June 2024, when Sir Keir came to power, to 208,000 in the year to March 2026.
paragraphs.zh: 基尔爵士的方案很直接：多建房子。他的政府在为期五年的任期内定下目标，要在英格兰每年建成30万套住房——住房政策在各英国议会（苏格兰、威尔士和北爱）间是下放管理的。政府推出了受欢迎的改革，包括对强制住房配额做出调整，以及修改规则，让靠近交通便利火车站的区域开发更容易获得支持。但其中一些改革最快也要到今年晚些时候才会落地，而且即便如此仍不够。按我们的计算，住房存量新增量从科尔上台时2024年6月年度的22.2万套，降到2026年3月年度的20.8万套。

paragraphs.en: The biggest reason is that, while the government freed one hand from planning restrictions, it tied down the other. A slew of fresh taxes and regulations—from a building-safety levy to new climate standards—have added to the cost and complexity of construction. Add to that an increase in the price of labour and materials, and the Home Builders Federation, a lobby group, estimates that construction costs for a typical home have risen by £76,000 over the past five years (equivalent to 20% of the average sale price), causing a “viability crunch”. A new rule requiring second staircases in buildings over seven storeys is halting the construction of new-build flats, particularly in London. Construction began on just 7,500 private homes in the capital last year, against a target of 88,000.
paragraphs.zh: 最主要的原因在于，政府一手放松了规划限制，另一手却又加了束缚。新近的一连串税费和规制——从建筑安全附加费到新的气候标准——都推高了建造成本和复杂度。再加上人工和材料价格上涨，住房建筑商联盟（Home Builders Federation）这一行业游说团体估算，过去五年里一栋普通住房的建造成本已上升了7.6万英镑（相当于平均销售价格的20%），并引发了“可持续性压力”。一项要求七层以上建筑必须新增第二道楼梯的规定也在遏制新建公寓，尤其在伦敦。去年伦敦仅开工7,500套私人住宅，远低于8.8万套的目标。

paragraphs.en: If Mr Burnham is serious about building more homes, further planning reforms and regulatory changes would be the easiest and cheapest way to do so. He should also look at the inefficient allocation of existing council homes. Attempting to build more council homes is a far inferior solution. The first problem is funding. The government has allocated £39bn over the next decade to build 300,000 affordable homes, including 18,000 social-rent homes, each year. The National Housing Federation, another industry lobby, reckons it costs £183,000 on average to build a social-rent home. If Mr Burnham earmarked all the current funds, perhaps 21,000 social-rent homes a year could be built. That suggests raising output to 75,000 homes a year would cost the government an extra £10bn annually (equivalent to more than a penny on every rate of income tax).
paragraphs.zh: 如果伯纳姆先生认真想要多建房子，那么进一步推进规划改革和监管调整将是最简单、最省成本的路径。他还应关注现有公房的配置效率。单纯尝试建设更多公屋并不是更优方案。首要问题是资金。政府已在未来十年内拨款390亿英镑，用于每年建设30万套可负担住房，其中包括1.8万套社会租赁住房。另一个行业游说组织全国住房联合会（National Housing Federation）估计，建造一套社会租赁住房平均要花18.3万英镑。即使伯纳姆先生把全部现有资金都拿来拨付，按这个算法，每年也许只能建约2.1万套社会租赁房。也就是说，要把产能提高到每年7.5万套，政府每年将额外支出约100亿英镑（相当于对每一英镑所得税税率都再加超过1便士）。

paragraphs.en: Finding such sums will be difficult given the pressures on spending. Another option would be to increase the share of homes that developers are required to set aside for affordable tenures in return for planning permission for private projects. Developers complain that these obligations—amounting to 15% of homes, on average—slow the building process and impair viability. Sir Sadiq Khan, London’s Labour mayor and a social-housing enthusiast, reluctantly reduced the obligations in the capital from 35% to 20% to unlock stalled schemes.
paragraphs.zh: 考虑到财政支出压力，筹集如此巨额资金将会很困难。另一个选择是，对开发商批准私人项目的规划许可附带条件，要求其划出一定比例的房屋用于可负担住房。开发商抱怨，这类附加义务——平均约占15%的住房——会拖慢建设进程并影响项目可行性。伦敦工党市长、社会住房倡导者西迪克·汗为了推动积压的项目，勉强把首都这类义务从35%下调到20%。

paragraphs.en: Mr Burnham has said that he can reduce the costs of his ambitious plans by making use of “vacant public land”. That seems fanciful. The 2015 Conservative government tried something similar, with a target of building 160,000 homes over five years on public land. In practice, the state had little understanding about the land it held and much of it required expensive remediation. Only a fraction of the target was reached before being quietly dropped.
paragraphs.zh: 伯纳姆还表示，他可以通过利用“闲置公共土地”来降低其雄心勃勃计划的成本。这听起来有些不切实际。2015年保守党政府也尝试过类似做法，目标是在五年内利用公共土地建造16万套住房。实际上，政府对自己持有的土地了解不够，且其中很多地块需要高昂的修复成本。这个目标只完成了一小部分，之后便被悄然搁置。

paragraphs.en: And then there are concerns about whether local authorities can even deliver all these houses. They have not built at scale since the 1970s and no longer employ the armies of architects and structural engineers to do so. Outsourcing that expertise is likely to be expensive and hiring their own experts will take councils time, which Mr Burnham does not have.
paragraphs.zh: 然后还存在一个问题：地方政府是否有能力交付这些住房。自20世纪70年代以来，它们就没有大规模建房了，也不再拥有庞大的建筑师和结构工程师队伍来承担这些工作。把这些专业能力外包出去可能十分昂贵，而让地方政府自行招募专家也会耗费大量时间，而伯纳姆显然没有那么多时间。

paragraphs.en: The most defensible part of his ambitious council-housing push concerns the loss of the existing stock. In 1980 Thatcher gave tenants the “right to buy” their homes at a generous discount; and nearly 2m have since been sold on the cheap. The current government already intends to make the discounts less generous (capping them at 15%). Mr Burnham, who laments the sale of council housing, will probably push this policy through.
paragraphs.zh: 他那项雄心勃勃的市政住房推进中，最站得住脚的一点是对现有存量房源流失的追溯。1980年，撒切尔夫人向租户提供了“以优惠价格购买自住房”的权利，于是此后近200万套住房被低价出售。现任政府已经计划将这种折扣幅度降下来，将其上限定为15%。伯纳姆一直在痛斥市政住房出售，预计他会推动这项政策继续落实。

paragraphs.en: But when it comes to building new homes, he needs only to look north to see the fruits that a housebuilding programme led by the private sector can bring. Under his watch as mayor of Greater Manchester housing supply increased by 61% over nine years thanks to better local co-ordination, a pragmatic approach to high-rise building and not burdening the private sector with excessive affordable-housing obligations. Indeed Sir Richard Leese, the former leader of the city council, said in 2021 that if the council had tried to impose such requirements, ”It would have delivered no housing at all, zero.” If Mr Burnham wants to solve the housing crisis he should practise Manchesterism.■
paragraphs.zh: 但当谈到建设新住房时，他只需向北方看一眼，就能看到由私营部门主导的住宅建设计划所带来的成果。在他担任大曼彻斯特市长期间，在九年间，由于当地协调更顺畅、对高层建筑采取务实态度，并且没有让私营部门背负过重的经济适用房义务，住房供应量增长了61%。事实上，前任市议会领袖理查德·利斯爵士在2021年就说过，如果议会试图强加这些要求，“那将一套房都建不出来，结果会是零”。如果伯纳姆先生想要解决住房危机，就应该实践“曼彻斯特主义”。

paragraphs.en: For more expert analysis of the biggest stories in Britain, sign up to Blighty, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 如果你想获得更多关于英国重大新闻的专家深度解读，请订阅《Blighty》，我们的周刊会员专享通讯。
